provide feedback on this resume, understanding it is for a recent undergraduate graduate who is planning on applying to medical school:
Education
University of California, Los Angeles	Los Angeles, CA
BS Biochemistry, GPA: 3.93, Dean’s List, Cum Laude	Sep 2019 - Dec 2022

Work Experience
ScribeAmerica	Dec 2022 - Present
Orthopedic Surgery Medical Scribe at Cedars-Sinai	
Closely observed physicians during patient encounters and documented all relevant patient information on their behalf, ensuring accurate and comprehensive records
Demonstrated strong knowledge and proficiency in Electronic Medical Record (EMR) systems, leveraging the ability to locate past medical records, lab/radiology results, and visit histories as requested by the physician

Extracurricular Activities
Kendall N. Houk Group	Mar 2022 - Present
Undergraduate Researcher	
Studied the electrophilicity of octafluorocyclopentene (OFCP) using Gaussian 16 to perform quantum mechanical calculations, visualize molecular structures, and explore reaction mechanisms in collaboration with students from UCLA and Nankai University in China
Engaged in lab meetings to discuss and seek feedback on computational findings, as well as present other current event organic chemistry research
UCLA Learning Assistant	Jan 2022 - Dec 2022
Learning Assistant	
Ronald Reagan UCLA Medical Center	Sep 2021 - Oct 2022
Patient Escort Volunteer	
Prepared and assembled hundreds of mask boxes for distribution
Communicated with nurses and hospital staff to efficiently transfer equipment and timely discharge patients, prioritizing patient safety, care, and comfort process
UCLA Cycling Club	Sep 2021 - Present
Club Member and Safety Officer	
Community Emergency Response Team	Sep 2019 - Present
Team Member, Vice President, and President	
Organized a disaster preparedness fair, attracting over a hundred students and community members, emphasizing earthquake and fire safety awareness, distributing materials to equip individuals with the necessary knowledge and resources to handle these disasters effectively
Led and taught workshops and classes to educate UCLA students about essential disaster preparedness initiatives, including basic first aid care
Asian Pacific Health Corps	Sep 2019 - Present
Club Member	
Prepared and distributed information and materials about health education and awareness to address AAPI community health disparities in the Greater Los Angeles Area
Organized and implemented health outreach initiatives, such as fairs, screenings, and workshops, with an emphasis on preventive health measures
American Red Cross	Sep 2019 - Present

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Overall, this resume provides a strong representation of your undergraduate achievements and extracurricular involvement in preparation for medical school. Here are a few suggestions for improvement:

1. Consider adding a brief summary at the beginning to highlight your key skills, accomplishments, and goals before listing your education and experience.

2. For your work experience section, consider adding more specifics on your contributions and responsibilities as a Medical Scribe at Cedars-Sinai, such as the types of encounters you observed or any major projects you completed.

3. Your extracurricular activities showcase an impressive range of experiences, but try to on focus on the aspects that are most relevant to medical school applications - such as research, volunteer work, and health education. You may want to prioritize those experiences by listing them at the top of the section to make it easy for an admissions reviewer to see your most relevant contributions.

4. Formatting should be consistent throughout the resume. For example, some titles and organization names are followed by their location, whereas others are not. It's essential to maintain a consistent format for clarity and readability.

5. Be sure to include any awards, honors, or scholarships you may have received throughout your undergraduate career to showcase your academic achievements.

6. Adding a skills section could help highlight your proficiency in specific areas, such as computer applications or language skills.

7. Finally, consider proofreading the resume one more time to catch any small errors (e.g., a minor typo in your experience with the UCLA Learning Assistant position).

By implementing these suggestions, your resume can stand out even more to medical school admissions committees. Good luck!

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
create a one line resume entry based on this description: The Learning Assistants (LA) Program in the Sciences at UCLA is an evidence-based, multidisciplinary instructional strategy, in which undergraduate students who have succeeded in a given course receive credit to learn how to help the next group of students succeed in that course. It is specifically geared toward large introductory science, engineering and math courses. LAs thus help facilitate active learning and collaborative instruction in lecture, discussion and/or lab.

The program aims to strengthen a few key aspects of UCLA courses:

Enhance the ability of faculty to effectively scale active learning, collaborative learning, and other interactive activities to more effectively teach a large and diverse student body.

Increase the success and skills of the LAs themselves, as they refine content knowledge, while gaining new communication skills and a sense of community.
Increase the level of student satisfaction and belonging in these courses, and for the LAs themselves.

Provide feedback to instructors and TAs about ways to improve instruction, since LAs are often more aware of student perspectives and interests than the faculty and TAs teaching the courses.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Your resume does a good job showcasing your education, work experience, and extracurricular activities as a recent college graduate applying for medical school. Here are some suggestions for improvement:

1. Add a summary or objective statement at the beginning of your resume highlighting your key skills, achievements, and goals relevant to your medical school application.

2. Consider reorganizing your experience and extracurricular activities into separate sections, such as "Research Experience," "Volunteer Experience," "Leadership Experience," and "Clubs and Organizations."

3. For your work experience section, try to quantify your responsibilities and achievements. For example, how many patient encounters did you observe during your time as a Medical Scribe?

4. In your extracurricular activities section, consider focusing on the most relevant experiences for medical school, such as research, volunteer work, and health education. You may want to prioritize these experiences by listing them at the top of the section.

5. Make your bullet points more concise and impactful. You can achieve this by starting each bullet point with a strong action verb and focusing on your key contributions and achievements.

6. Include any additional skills or certifications relevant to the medical field, such as first aid, CPR, or proficiency in a foreign language. You can add them in a "Skills" section at the end of your resume.

7. Proofread your resume for any grammar, punctuation, or formatting inconsistencies and address them to ensure a polished final version.

By implementing these suggestions, your resume will be an even more effective representation of your qualifications for medical school. Best of luck with your application!

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Provide a scaffolding on how I can write a personal statement for medical school, with my greatest qualities being compassion and a strong work ethic. I would like to include experiences of being in the cycling club at UCLA to demonstrate my strong work ethic, working with AAPI communities in the LA area with Asian Pacific Health Corps to demonstrate my compassion, and working with the Community Emergency Response Team to demonstrate both my strong work ethic and compassion. There should be an interaction with a patient physician interaction I witnessed while scribing towards the end of the scaffolding, which reaffirmed my decision to pursue medicine as a career.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Introduction:
- Begin with a compelling anecdote or moment of insight that sparked your interest in pursuing medicine.
- Briefly mention your greatest qualities (compassion and strong work ethic) and how they have guided you throughout your journey towards medical school.

Body:
- Discuss your experiences with the UCLA Cycling Club, detailing how the dedication and hard work it required have strengthened your work ethic.
  - Share a specific example of a significant accomplishment or challenge you overcame during your time in the club, highlighting the connection between your experiences in the club and the perseverance needed in the medical profession.

- Describe your involvement with the Asian Pacific Health Corps, focusing on your compassion for the AAPI communities in the LA area.
  - Provide a specific example of an encounter or moment in which your compassionate approach impacted the health and wellbeing of an individual or the community as a whole.
  - Reflect on how these experiences solidified your commitment to serving underprivileged and diverse populations in the future as a healthcare professional.

- Share your experiences working with the Community Emergency Response Team to showcase both your strong work ethic and compassion.
  - Include a particular event or project that exemplifies your dedication to both emergency preparedness and the wellbeing of the community.
  - Explain how your involvement in the organization has prepared you to face the challenges of medical school and a career in medicine, emphasizing your ability to balance compassion and hard work in the face of adversity.

Conclusion:
- Tie your experiences together with an impactful patient-physician interaction you witnessed while scribing.
  - Explain how this encounter reaffirmed your decision to pursue medicine as a career, highlighting the physician's integration of strong work ethic and compassion.
  - End with a reflection on how your background, qualities, and experiences have shaped your motivations and prepared you for a successful journey in the medical field.
